title: Sarga 25
summary: Rama's divine birth, childhood, protection of Sage Vishwamitra's rituals from demons, breaking of Shiva's bow, and marriage to Sita. Addresses youth, childhood, education, overcoming early obstacles, and marriage.

sections.summary: This passage begins with “Having given that terrible forest to Khara and having consoled his sister, Dashagriva became delighted and more at ease.” and develops toward “O King, he also obtained two inexhaustible quivers with arrows, a bow that is very difficult to conquer, and a powerful weapon that destroys enemies…”

This passage begins with “Having obtained all these boons, O Dashanana, your son, now that the sacrifice is concluded, and I myself, stand here eager to behold you.” and develops toward “She, the daughter born of Anala, is our mother's sister's daughter, and thus by dharma, she is indeed our sister.”

This passage begins with “O King, she was abducted by the powerful demon Madhu while my son was engaged in a sacrifice and I was dwelling in penance underwater.” and develops toward “All the Rakshasas set forth, mounted on donkeys, camels, splendid horses, porpoises, and great serpents, completely filling the sky.”

This passage begins with “Hundreds of Daityas, who had long-standing enmity with the gods, saw Ravana going and indeed followed him from behind.” and develops toward “Therefore, O Rakshasa, you should go with your relatives to help him, for it is proper to provide assistance to an affectionate one who seeks it.”

This passage begins with “Having heard her words, Madhu replied, "So be it."” and develops toward “Then, the lord of Rakshasas, Ravana, who resembled Mahendra, reached Mount Kailasa, the abode of Kubera, and encamped his army there.”

verses.sanskrit: स तु दत्त्वा दशग्रीवो वनं घोरं स्वरस्य तत् । भगिनीं च समाश्वास्य हृष्टः स्वस्थतरो ऽभवत् ।। 7.25.1 ।।
verses.translation: Having given that terrible forest to Khara and having consoled his sister, Dashagriva became delighted and more at ease.

verses.sanskrit: ततो निकुम्भिला नाम लङ्कोपवनमुत्तमम् । तद्राक्षसेन्द्रो बलवान्प्रविवेश सहानुगः ।। 7.25.2 ।।
verses.translation: Then, the powerful king of Rakshasas, Ravana, entered that excellent pleasure grove of Lanka named Nikumbhila, along with his followers.

verses.sanskrit: ततो यूपशताकीर्णं सौम्यचैत्योपशोभितम् । ददर्श विष्ठितं यज्ञं श्रिया सम्प्रज्वलन्निव ।। 7.25.3 ।।
verses.translation: Then, he saw the established sacrifice, which was filled with hundreds of sacrificial posts and adorned with auspicious altars, appearing as if it was blazing forth with splendor.

verses.sanskrit: ततः कृष्णाजिनधरं कमण्डलुशिखाध्वजम् । ददर्श स्वसुतं तत्र मेघनादं भयावहम् ।। 7.25.4 ।।
verses.translation: Then, he saw his own formidable son, Meghanada, there, wearing a black deer skin and distinguished by his water-pot and top-knot.

verses.sanskrit: तं समासाद्य लङ्केशः परिष्वज्वाथ बाहुभिः । अब्रवीत्किमिदं वत्स वर्तसे ब्रूहि तत्त्वतः ।। 7.25.5 ।।
verses.translation: Having approached and embraced him with his arms, Ravana, the lord of Lanka, asked, "My son, what is this you are doing? Tell me the truth."

verses.sanskrit: उशना त्वब्रवीत्तत्र यज्ञसम्पत्समृद्धये । रावणं राक्षसश्रेष्ठं द्विजश्रेष्ठो महातपाः ।। 7.25.6 ।।
verses.translation: There, Ushana, the best of Brahmins and of great penance, spoke to Ravana, the best of Rakshasas, regarding the successful completion of the sacrifice.

verses.sanskrit: अहमाख्यामि ते राजञ्छ्रूयतां सर्वमेव तत् । यज्ञास्ते सप्त पुत्रेण प्राप्तास्सुबहुविस्तराः ।। 7.25.7 ।।
verses.translation: Ushana then said, "O King, I will tell you all about it; listen, for your son has completed seven very extensive sacrifices."

verses.sanskrit: अग्निष्टोमो ऽश्वमेधश्च यज्ञो बहुसुवर्णकः । राजसूयस्तथा यज्ञो गोमेधो वैष्ववस्तथा ।। 7.25.8 ।।
verses.translation: The Agnishtoma, Ashwamedha, Bahusuvarnaka, Rajasuya, Gomedha, and Vaishvava are all types of sacrifices.

verses.sanskrit: माहेश्वरे प्रवृत्ते तु यज्ञे पुम्भिः सुदुर्लभे । वरांस्ते लब्धवान्पुत्रः साक्षात्पशुपतेरिह ।। 7.25.9 ।।
verses.translation: When the Maheswara sacrifice, which is very difficult for men to perform, was commenced, your son directly obtained boons from Pashupati (Lord Shiva) here.

verses.sanskrit: कामगं स्यन्दनं दिव्यमन्तरिक्षचरं ध्रुवम् । मायां च तामसीं नाम यया सम्पद्यते तमः ।। 7.25.10 ।।
verses.translation: He obtained a divine chariot that moves at will, travels in the sky, and is firm, and also an illusion named Tamasi, by which darkness is produced.

verses.sanskrit: एतया किल सङ्ग्रामे मायया राक्षसेश्वर । प्रयुक्तया गतिः शक्या नहि ज्ञातुं सुरासुरैः ।। 7.25.11 ।।
verses.translation: O Lord of Rakshasas, it is said that when this illusion is employed in battle, its movement cannot indeed be ascertained by even gods and asuras.

verses.sanskrit: अक्षयाविषुधी बाणैश्चापं चापि सुदुर्जयम् । अस्त्रं च बलवद्राजञ्छत्रुविध्वंसनं रणे ।। 7.25.12 ।।
verses.translation: O King, he also obtained two inexhaustible quivers with arrows, a bow that is very difficult to conquer, and a powerful weapon that destroys enemies in battle.

verses.sanskrit: एतान्सर्वान्वरांल्लब्ध्वा पुत्रस्ते ऽयं दशानन । अद्य यज्ञसमाप्तौ च त्वां दिदृक्षुस्स्थितो ह्यहम् ।। 7.25.13 ।।
verses.translation: Having obtained all these boons, O Dashanana, your son, now that the sacrifice is concluded, and I myself, stand here eager to behold you.

verses.sanskrit: ततो ऽब्रवीदृशग्रीवो न शोभनमिदं कृतम् । पूजिताः शत्रवो यस्माद्द्रव्यैरिन्द्रपुरोगमाः ।। 7.25.14 ।।
verses.translation: Then Dashagriva spoke, saying that this was not good because the enemies, led by Indra, had been honored with wealth.

verses.sanskrit: एहीदानीं कृतं विद्धि सुकृतं तन्न संशयः । आगच्छ सौम्य गच्छामः स्वमेव भवनं प्रति ।। 7.25.15 ।।
verses.translation: Come now, know that what is done is well done, there is no doubt about it; come, O gentle one, let us go to our own abode.

verses.sanskrit: ततो गत्वा दशग्रीवः सपुत्रः सविभीषणः । स्त्रियो ऽवतारयामास सर्वास्ता बाष्पगद्गदाः ।। 7.25.16 ।।
verses.translation: Then, having gone, Dashagriva, along with his sons and Vibhishana, brought down all those women who were choked with tears.

verses.sanskrit: लक्षिण्यो रत्नभूताश्च देवदानवरक्षसाम् । तस्य तासु मतिं ज्ञात्वा धर्मत्मा वाक्यमब्रवीत् ।। 7.25.17 ।।
verses.translation: Knowing his intention towards those auspicious, jewel-like women of gods, Danavas, and Rakshasas, the righteous one (Vibhishana) spoke these words.

verses.sanskrit: ईदृशैस्त्वं समाचारैर्यशोर्थकुलनाशनैः । धर्षणं ज्ञातिनां ज्ञात्वा स्वमतेन विचेष्टसे ।। 7.25.18 ।।
verses.translation: You act by your own will with such conduct that destroys fame, wealth, and family, even though you know it is an outrage to your kinsmen.

verses.sanskrit: ज्ञातींस्तान्धर्षयित्वेमास्त्वया ऽ ऽनीता वराङ्गनाः । त्वामतिक्रम्य मधुना राजन्कुम्भीनसी हृता ।। 7.25.19 ।।
verses.translation: O King, while you brought these beautiful women after humiliating their kinsmen, Madhu has abducted Kumbhinasi, disregarding your authority.

verses.sanskrit: रावणस्त्वब्रवीद्वाक्यं नावगच्छामि किं त्विदम् । को ऽयं यस्तु त्वया ऽ ऽख्यातो मधुरित्येव नामतः ।। 7.25.20 ।।
verses.translation: Ravana then spoke, saying, "I do not understand what this is; who is this person named Madhu whom you have mentioned?"

verses.sanskrit: विभीषणस्तु सङ्क्रुद्धो भ्रातरं वाक्यमब्रवीत् । श्रूयतामस्य पापस्य कर्मणः फलमागतम् ।। 7.25.21 ।।
verses.translation: But Vibhishana, greatly enraged, spoke to his brother, saying, "Listen, the consequence of this sinful deed has arrived."

verses.sanskrit: मातामहस्य यो भ्राता ज्येष्ठो भ्राता सुमालिनः । माल्यवानिति विख्यातो वृद्धः प्राज्ञो निशाचरः ।। 7.25.22 ।।
verses.translation: Malyavan, the elder brother of Sumali, who is our maternal grandfather's brother, is a renowned, old, and wise Rakshasa.

verses.sanskrit: पिता ज्येष्ठो जनन्या नो ह्यस्माकं चार्यको ऽभवत् । तस्य कुम्भीनसी नाम दुहितुर्दुहिता ऽभवत् ।। 7.25.23 ।।
verses.translation: He was indeed the elder father of our mother, and thus our maternal grandfather, and his daughter's daughter was named Kumbhinasi.

verses.sanskrit: मातृष्वसुरथास्माकं सा च कन्या ऽनलोद्भवा । भवत्यस्माकमेवैषा भ्रातृणां धर्मतः स्वसा ।। 7.25.24 ।।
verses.translation: She, the daughter born of Anala, is our mother's sister's daughter, and thus by dharma, she is indeed our sister.

verses.sanskrit: सा हृता, मधुना राजन्राक्षसेन बलीयसा । यज्ञप्रवृत्ते पुत्रे तु मयि चान्तर्जलोषिते ।। 7.25.25 ।।
verses.translation: O King, she was abducted by the powerful demon Madhu while my son was engaged in a sacrifice and I was dwelling in penance underwater.

verses.sanskrit: कुम्भकर्णे महाराज निद्रामनुभवत्यथ । निहत्य राक्षसश्रेष्ठानमात्यानिह सम्मतान् ।। 7.25.26 ।।
verses.translation: O great king, at that time, while Kumbhakarna was experiencing his long sleep, Madhu killed the best and most respected demon ministers here.

verses.sanskrit: धर्षयित्वा हृता सा तु सुप्ताप्यन्तःपुरे तव । श्रुत्वा ऽपि तन्महाराज क्षान्तमेव हतो न सः ।। 7.25.27 ।।
verses.translation: She was abducted after being violated, even while sleeping in your inner chambers, yet, O great king, even after hearing of that, he was indeed forgiven and not killed.

verses.sanskrit: यस्मादवश्यं दातव्या कन्या भर्त्रे हि भ्रातृभिः । तदेतत्कर्मणो ह्यस्य फलं पापस्य दुर्मते ।। 7.25.28 ।।
verses.translation: Because a daughter must indeed be given to her husband by her brothers, this, O evil-minded one, is truly the consequence of his sinful act.

verses.sanskrit: अस्मिन्नेवाभिसम्प्राप्तं लोके विदितमस्तु ते । विभीषणवचः श्रुत्वा राक्षसेन्द्रः स रावणः । दौरात्म्येनात्मनोद्धूतस्तप्ताम्भ इव सागरः ।। 7.25.29 ।।
verses.translation: Let it be known to you that upon hearing Vibhishana's words, that king of demons Ravana was agitated by his own wickedness, like an ocean stirred by hot water.

verses.sanskrit: ततो ऽब्रवीदृशग्रीवः क्रुद्धः संरक्तलोचनः । कल्प्यतां मे रथः शीघ्रं शूराः सज्जीभवन्तु नः ।। 7.25.30 ।।
verses.translation: Then, Ravana, enraged and with reddened eyes, spoke, saying, "Let my chariot be prepared quickly, and let our brave warriors be ready."

verses.sanskrit: भ्राता मे कुम्भकर्णश्च ये च मुख्या निशाचराः । वाहनान्यधिरोहन्तु नानाप्रहरणायुधाः ।। 7.25.31 ।।
verses.translation: My brother Kumbhakarna and the other chief demons, armed with various weapons, should mount their vehicles.

verses.sanskrit: अद्य तं समरे हत्वा मधुं रावणनिर्भयम् । सुरलोकं गमिष्यामि युद्धकाङ्क्षी सुहृद्वृतः ।। 7.25.32 ।।
verses.translation: Today, having killed Madhu in battle, who is fearless of Ravana, I shall go to the world of gods, desirous of battle and surrounded by my friends.

verses.sanskrit: अक्षौहिणीसहस्राणि चत्वार्यग्र्याणि रक्षसाम् । नानाप्रहरणान्याशु निर्ययुर्युद्धकाङ्क्षिणाम् ।। 7.25.33 ।।
verses.translation: Four thousand foremost Akshauhinis of demons, armed with various weapons and eager for battle, quickly set out.

verses.sanskrit: इन्द्रजित्त्वग्रतः (अग्रतः) सैन्यात्सैनिकान्परिगृह्य च । जगाम रावणो मध्ये कुम्भकर्णश्च पृष्ठतः ।। 7.25.34 ।।
verses.translation: Indrajit led the army from the front, Ravana marched in the middle, and Kumbhakarna followed at the rear.

verses.sanskrit: विभीषणश्च धर्मात्मा लङ्कायां धर्मामाचरत् । शोषाः सर्वे महाभागा ययुर्मधुपुरं प्रति ।। 7.25.35 ।।
verses.translation: The righteous Vibhishana remained in Lanka practicing dharma, while all the other illustrious remaining ones went towards Madhupura.

verses.sanskrit: खरैरुष्ट्रैर्हयैर्दीप्तैः शिंशुमारैर्महोरगैः । राक्षसाः प्रययुः सर्वे कृत्वा ऽ ऽकाशं निरन्तरम् ।। 7.25.36 ।।
verses.translation: All the Rakshasas set forth, mounted on donkeys, camels, splendid horses, porpoises, and great serpents, completely filling the sky.

verses.sanskrit: दैत्याश्च शतशस्तत्र कृतवैराश्च दैवतैः । रावणं प्रेक्ष्य गच्छन्तमन्वगच्छन्हि पृष्ठतः ।। 7.25.37 ।।
verses.translation: Hundreds of Daityas, who had long-standing enmity with the gods, saw Ravana going and indeed followed him from behind.

verses.sanskrit: स तु गत्वा मधुपुरं प्रविश्य च दशाननः । न ददर्श मधुं तत्र भगिनीं तत्र दृष्टवान् ।। 7.25.38 ।।
verses.translation: Having gone to Madhupura and entered it, Ravana did not find Madhu there, but instead saw his sister.

verses.sanskrit: सा च प्रह्वाञ्जलिर्भूत्वा शिरसा चरणौ गता ।। 7.25.39 ।।
verses.translation: And she, becoming humble with folded hands, touched his feet with her head.

verses.sanskrit: तस्य राक्षसराजस्य त्रस्ता कुम्भीनसी तदा । तां समुत्थापयामास न भेतव्यमिति ब्रुवन् ।। 7.25.40 ।।
verses.translation: Then, Kumbhinasi, frightened by that king of Rakshasas, made her rise, saying, "You should not be afraid."

verses.sanskrit: रावणो राक्षसश्रेष्ठः किं चापि करवाणि ते । साब्रवीद्यदि मे राजन्प्रसन्नस्त्वं महाभुज । भर्तारं न ममेहाद्य हन्तुर्महसि मानद ।। 7.25.41 ।।
verses.translation: Ravana, O best of Rakshasas, asked, "What else shall I do for you?" She replied, "If you are pleased with me, O king, O mighty-armed one, O giver of honor, then you should not kill my husband here today."

verses.sanskrit: न हीदृशं भयं किञ्चित्कुलस्त्रीणामिहोच्यते । भयानामपि सर्वेषां वैधव्यं व्यसनं महत् ।। 7.25.42 ।।
verses.translation: Indeed, no such fear is spoken of for noble women in this world, for among all fears, widowhood is the greatest calamity.

verses.sanskrit: सत्यवाग्भव राजेन्द्र मामवेक्षस्व याचतीम् । त्वयाप्युक्तं महाराज न भेतव्यमिति स्वयम् ।। 7.25.43 ।।
verses.translation: O king of kings, be truthful in your word and consider me, who is begging, for you yourself, O great king, said, "You should not be afraid."

verses.sanskrit: रावणस्त्वब्रवीद्धृष्टः स्वसारं तत्र संस्थिताम् । क्व चासौ तव भर्ता वै मम शीघ्रं निवेद्यताम् ।। 7.25.44 ।।
verses.translation: Impudent Ravana said to his sister, who was standing there, "Where is your husband? Show him to me quickly."

verses.sanskrit: सह तेन गमिष्यामि सुरलोकं जयावहे । तव कारुण्यसौहार्दान्निवृत्तो ऽस्मि मधोर्वधात् ।। 7.25.45 ।।
verses.translation: I will go with him to conquer the world of the gods, and because of your compassion and friendship, I have refrained from killing Madhu.

verses.sanskrit: इत्युक्ता सा समुत्थाप्य प्रसुप्तं तं निशाचरम् । अब्रवीत्सम्प्रहृष्टेव राक्षसी सा पतिं वचः ।। 7.25.46 ।।
verses.translation: Thus addressed, she, the female demon, awakened her sleeping husband, the night-wanderer, and spoke words to him as if greatly delighted.

verses.sanskrit: एष प्राप्तो दशग्रीवो मम भ्राता महाबलः । सुरलोकजयाकाङ्क्षी साहाय्ये त्वां वृणोति च ।। 7.25.47 ।।
verses.translation: My very powerful brother Dashagriva has arrived, desirous of conquering the world of the gods, and he requests your assistance.

verses.sanskrit: तदस्य त्वं सहायार्थं सबन्धुर्गच्छ राक्षस । स्निग्धस्य भजमानस्य युक्तमर्थाय कल्पितुम् ।। 7.25.48 ।।
verses.translation: Therefore, O Rakshasa, you should go with your relatives to help him, for it is proper to provide assistance to an affectionate one who seeks it.

verses.sanskrit: तस्यास्तद्वचनं श्रुत्वा तथेत्याह मधुर्वचः ।। 7.25.49 ।।
verses.translation: Having heard her words, Madhu replied, "So be it."

verses.sanskrit: ददर्श राक्षसश्रेष्ठं यथान्यायमुपेत्य सः । पूजयमास धर्मेण रावणं राक्षसाधिपम् ।। 7.25.50 ।।
verses.translation: He approached Ravana, the best and lord of Rakshasas, and worshipped him according to custom and righteousness.

verses.sanskrit: प्राप्य पूजां दशग्रीवो मधुवेश्मानि वीर्यवान् । तत्र चैकां निशामुष्य गमनायोपचक्रमे ।। 7.25.51 ।।
verses.translation: The powerful Ravana, having received worship in Madhu's abode and stayed there for one night, then prepared for his departure.

verses.sanskrit: ततः कैलासमासाद्य शैलं वैश्रवणालयम् । राक्षसेन्द्रो महेन्द्राभः सेनामुपनिवेशयत् ।। 7.25.52 ।।
verses.translation: Then, the lord of Rakshasas, Ravana, who resembled Mahendra, reached Mount Kailasa, the abode of Kubera, and encamped his army there.
